archimate:Goal.name: Performance Testing and Tuning
archimate:Goal.documentation:
Definition: Architecture Decision Record from where you should specialise the ADR SBBs regarding the Performance Testing and Tuning
Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022
Source reference: https://www.iso.org/standard/74393.html
Additional information: Performance testing and tuning is a decision of IT architecture that involves assessing the behavior of a system or application under different conditions and identifying areas where improvements can be made. This process helps to ensure that the system or application can handle the expected workload and provide a satisfactory user experience. Performance testing and tuning can involve a range of techniques, including load testing, stress testing, and capacity planning. It is an ongoing process that requires regular monitoring and adjustment to ensure optimal performance.
Example: Performance Testing and Tuning: Decision: Conducting performance testing and tuning exercises regularly to identify bottlenecks and optimize system performance. Rationale: Performance testing helps uncover performance issues and provides insights into system limitations, enabling public administrations to make informed decisions on infrastructure scaling, code optimization, and configuration adjustments to enhance performance.
LOST view: Digital Solution Architecture Decisions Catalogue view
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/PerformanceTestingAndTuningGoal
ABB name: eira:PerformanceTestingAndTuningGoal
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2024-01-28
Identifier: ADR-20230515180947440
Name: Architecture Decision Record about Performance Testing and Tuning
eira:adr_context: The context explains why we need to make a decision. It also describes the alternatives along with the pros and cons.
eira:adr_decision: The decision describes the justification for why the particular solution was accepted. It has more emphasis on the why rather than the how.
eira:adr_status: [Proposed (under review)|Accepted (approved and ready for implementation)|Superseded (superseded by another decision)]
eira:adr_consecuences: The consequences section contains information about the overall impact of an architectural decision. Every decision has trade-offs. That’s why it’s crucial to include the analysis to provide a clear picture.
Interoperability Layer: N/A
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DigitalSolutionArchitectureDecisionGoal

properties.value: Architecture Decision Record about Performance Testing and Tuning

Performance Testing and Tuning: Decision: Conducting performance testing and tuning exercises regularly to identify bottlenecks and optimize system performance. Rationale: Performance testing helps uncover performance issues and provides insights into system limitations, enabling public administrations to make informed decisions on infrastructure scaling, code optimization, and configuration adjustments to enhance performance.
The context explains why we need to make a decision. It also describes the alternatives along with the pros and cons.
The decision describes the justification for why the particular solution was accepted. It has more emphasis on the why rather than the how.
[Proposed (under review)|Accepted (approved and ready for implementation)|Superseded (superseded by another decision)]

The consequences section contains information about the overall impact of an architectural decision. Every decision has trade-offs. That’s why it’s crucial to include the analysis to provide a clear picture.

Performance testing and tuning is a decision of IT architecture that involves assessing the behavior of a system or application under different conditions and identifying areas where improvements can be made. This process helps to ensure that the system or application can handle the expected workload and provide a satisfactory user experience. Performance testing and tuning can involve a range of techniques, including load testing, stress testing, and capacity planning. It is an ongoing process that requires regular monitoring and adjustment to ensure optimal performance.
Architecture Decision Record from where you should specialise the ADR SBBs regarding the Performance Testing and Tuning
Digital Solution Architecture Decisions Catalogue view
